reasoning: The comment at L253 states 'The server now returns a success response with error message' immediately before expect(content.success).toBe(false). At the tool-payload level 'success response' directly contradicts success: false — a failure payload — and the same pattern recurs at L235/L276 in-file and in server-control-tools.test.ts (L187, L289, L341), supporting confirmed drift with an actionable reword ('tool-level failure payload' / 'non-throwing error response'). Accepted as gray under the panel resolve-to-gray protocol: the opposing protocol-level lens (see gray_reason) is genuinely defensible, so the case is real data but excluded from headline metrics.
gray_reason: Protocol-level reading is defensible: the comment contrasts throwing McpError with returning a non-throwing (protocol-successful) response that carries an error message — at the MCP envelope level 'success response with error message' accurately describes the changed behavior, making this born-imprecise wording rather than drift.
